Title: xCatalog — Search Products Query
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. Products indexed in B2B-store; B2B virtual catalog root active (@td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id)). @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) resolves to B2B-store.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=search_products] errors[] empty
[DATA label=search_products] data.products is non-null
[DATA label=search_products] data.products.totalCount >= 0
[DATA label=search_products] data.products.items is non-null
[DATA label=search_products] data.products.pageInfo is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute same query → same totalCount returned
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; data.products null; DV-008 on field names; HTTP 500
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — Query.products; Migrated from legacy browser-mode (TLC-2026-05-15-2249, 2026-05-15)

Title: xCatalog — Get Product Detail Query
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. Capture-chains from CAT-GQL-001 pattern: first queries products to discover a real product id, then queries product() for full PDP fields. @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) resolves to B2B-store.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=discover_product] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=get_product] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_product] data.product is non-null
[DATA label=get_product] data.product.id = {{PROD_ID}}
[DATA label=get_product] data.product.name is non-null
[DATA label=get_product] data.product.price is non-null
[DATA label=get_product] data.product.availabilityData is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] Re-execute get_product with same PROD_ID → identical response
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; data.product null for real PROD_ID; name or price null; DV-008 on field names; HTTP 500
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — Query.product + Product type; Migrated from legacy browser-mode (TLC-2026-05-15-2249, 2026-05-15)

Title: xCatalog — category Singular Query by ID
Preconditions: Runner-native. Captures a real category id from categories query, then queries by id.
Steps:
[GQL-OP list_cats]
  query { categories(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" first: 1 cultureName: "en-US") { items { id } } }
[GQL-EXEC list_cats]
[GQL-CAPTURE list_cats.data.categories.items.0.id → CAT_ID]
[GQL-OP cat_one]
  query { category(id: "{{CAT_ID}}" storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" cultureName: "en-US") { id code name level slug } }
[GQL-EXEC cat_one]
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=cat_one] errors[] empty
[DATA label=cat_one] data.category is non-null
[DATA label=cat_one] data.category.id = {{CAT_ID}}
[DATA label=cat_one] data.category.name is non-null
Failure_Signals: category returns null for valid id; name missing

Title: xCatalog — products Cursor Pagination
Preconditions: Runner-native. Requests first=2 then first=2 after endCursor. Verifies pagination returns different items via cursor.
Steps:
[GQL-OP page1]
  query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" currencyCode: "USD" first: 2 filter: "category.subtree:@td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id)") { totalCount pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } items { id } } }
[GQL-EXEC page1]
[GQL-CAPTURE page1.data.products.pageInfo.endCursor → CURSOR]
[GQL-OP page2]
  query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" currencyCode: "USD" first: 2 after: "{{CURSOR}}" filter: "category.subtree:@td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id)") { pageInfo { hasPreviousPage } items { id } } }
[GQL-EXEC page2]
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=page1] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=page2] errors[] empty
[DATA label=page1] data.products.pageInfo.endCursor is non-null
[DATA label=page2] data.products is non-null
Failure_Signals: Cursor pagination broken; same items returned on page 2

Title: [VCST-4173] xCatalog products term_facets Present After FilteredBrowsing Migration
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. Verifies term_facets[] is populated after VCST-4173 migrated FilteredBrowsing config from DynamicProperties to Store Settings. Uses active B2B virtual catalog root @td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id) (per feedback_graphql_products_filter memory).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=products_term_facets] errors[] empty
[DATA label=products_term_facets] data.products is non-null
[DATA label=products_term_facets] data.products.term_facets is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Confirm same facet groups appear in Admin > Catalog > Store > FilteredBrowsing blade as before migration
Failure_Signals: term_facets[] null or empty after migration (config lost); facet count drops to 0 where it was >0 pre-migration; HTTP 500; DV-008 on facet field names

Title: [VCST-4173] xCatalog Storefront Price Range Filter `[100 TO 200]` (Frontend-Truthful Syntax)
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. Replicates the exact filter the storefront sends when a shopper types/drags a price range in the catalog sidebar — `\"price\":[from TO to]` inside the `filter:` arg, alongside the always-present `price.USD:(0 TO)` base predicate. NO `facet:` arg (storefront never sends one — range_facets are server-driven by store FilteredBrowsing config). Verifies (a) totalCount narrows below baseline, (b) range_facets[] is populated with store-defined buckets, (c) the facet name is `price`. Live capture: reports/exploratory/SBTM-cart-2026-05-20/price-filter-capture.md.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=products_range_filter] errors[] empty
[DATA label=products_range_filter] data.products is non-null
[COUNT label=products_range_filter] data.products.totalCount > 0
[COUNT label=products_range_filter] data.products.range_facets.length >= 1
[DATA label=products_range_filter] data.products.range_facets.0.name = price
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Confirm Admin > Catalog > Store > FilteredBrowsing shows price.USD ranges persisted; verify same filter string drives the same totalCount when sent from the storefront SearchProducts query
Failure_Signals: range_facets[] empty after migration (range config not written to Store Settings); inline `\"price\"` literal filter syntax rejected; totalCount unchanged from baseline (filter not honored); range_facets[0].name != "price"; HTTP 500
References: VCST-4173; xAPI; Query.products range_facets; FilteredBrowsing; storefront /graphql SearchProducts

Failure_Signals: open-lower-bound syntax rejected; totalCount unchanged from baseline; range_facets[] empty

Title: xCatalog Price Filter — Wide Range `[0 TO 999999]` Should Match Baseline
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. A wide-enough range should not constrain results — totalCount should equal (or essentially equal) the baseline `price.USD:(0 TO)` totalCount. Sanity check that the filter is parsed correctly and not silently rejected. BVA: maximum-range case.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=price_wide_range] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=price_wide_range] data.products.totalCount > 0
[COUNT label=price_wide_range] data.products.range_facets.length >= 1
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Compare totalCount with a same-store baseline query without the price clause — should be within ±0 (boundary 999999 effectively excludes nothing)
Failure_Signals: totalCount drops dramatically (filter clamps unexpectedly); range_facets[] empty

Title: xCatalog Price Filter — Empty Intersection `[10000000 TO 99999999]` Should Return 0
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. A price range above the catalog max should return totalCount==0 with no errors. Verifies the server handles empty-intersection gracefully (not 500, not falling back to baseline). BVA: above-domain case. NOTE: 6 real-estate product listings in the B2B catalog reach ~$4.87M (Melbourne properties); the boundary is set to $10M–$100M to exceed all known data outliers.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=price_empty_intersection] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=price_empty_intersection] data.products.totalCount = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] No products priced above $999,999 should exist in the B2B catalog
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 on empty range; totalCount falls back to baseline (filter ignored); errors[] non-empty

Title: xCatalog Price Filter — Inverted Range `[200 TO 100]` (Negative Boundary)
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. from > to is logically invalid. Server may (a) return totalCount==0, (b) return errors[], or (c) silently swap. This case captures the actual behavior to baseline future regressions — assert no 500 and `range_facets` are still queryable.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=price_inverted] errors[] empty
[DATA label=price_inverted] data.products is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Document observed behavior (totalCount value) — server should not 500 on logically inverted range
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 / 400; data.products null; errors[] contains parser exception

Title: xCatalog Price Filter — Negative-From `[-1 TO 100]` (Negative Boundary)
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. Storefront UI silently rejects sub-min values (no GraphQL fired), but direct API callers may send negative floors. Baselines the server-side behavior — assert no 500 and totalCount is a valid count. BVA: below-domain case.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=price_negative_from] errors[] empty
[DATA label=price_negative_from] data.products is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Direct API callers may construct sub-zero floors; document server tolerance vs rejection
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500; data.products null

Title: xCatalog Price Filter — Full Storefront Chain `(0 TO) + [100 TO 200] + inStock_variations`
Preconditions: Runner-native. PUBLIC role. The complete filter the storefront sends in production — base predicate + user range + in-stock toggle. Verifies all three clauses cooperate (in-stock further narrows totalCount vs CAT-GQL-116). BVA: real-world combined filter.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=price_full_chain] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=price_full_chain] data.products.totalCount > 0
[COUNT label=price_full_chain] data.products.range_facets.length >= 1
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Compare totalCount with CAT-GQL-116 — the in-stock clause should narrow it further (or equal it if all are in-stock)
Failure_Signals: totalCount > CAT-GQL-116 (in-stock not honored); range_facets[] empty
